$comment: Lighthouse CI config — budgets + URLs. Floors declared here fail the build. Raise them only with justification (bundle-size style commit) — the point is to catch regressions, not document aspirations.
$comment-floors: Category-level floors. A regression below any of these fails CI. Thresholds are deliberately realistic for a dashboard SPA with live polling, not aspirational marketing numbers.
$comment-a11y-strict: a11y floor is 0.9 because we explicitly test axe in vitest + Playwright; a Lighthouse a11y miss here means there's a route-level issue the component-level tests didn't catch
$comment-perf-realistic: perf floor of 0.7 accounts for the live-polling overhead (useApiData fires fetches for every hook on mount). If we later move to a single SSE endpoint the floor should raise to 0.85
$comment-seo-realistic: seo floor is 0.8 because this is a self-hosted admin dashboard, not a public page — we don't need canonical URLs, OG tags, or crawl-optimized meta descriptions. Floor exists only to catch sudden regressions in title/lang/viewport basics.

settings.$comment-preset: desktop matches the audience: this is a devtool dashboard, not a public-consumer page
settings.$comment-skip: uses-http2 + canonical don't apply to a self-hosted localhost dashboard
